# Beatpoly — llms.txt
# https://beatpoly.com

## About
Beatpoly is a free prediction market trading education platform focused on Kalshi and Polymarket.
Tagline: "Stop guessing. Start harvesting."
Core thesis: 72% of prediction market contracts resolve NO. 87.3% of traders lose money. Systematic edge exists for those who trade the math, not the narrative.

## Brand Voice
- Ruthlessly rational. Calm and authoritative. No hype.
- Data-driven: every claim backed by academic research or platform data.
- Plain language: no jargon without explanation.
- The "anti-casino": we exploit crowd mispricings systematically.

## Core Statistics (sourced, verified)
- 72% of prediction market contracts resolve NO (161,000 contracts, Kalshi + Polymarket)
- 87.3% of Polymarket wallets are unprofitable (112,000 wallet study)
- Takers lose 32% on average vs Makers lose 10% (Bürgi, Deng & Whelan, 2026, 300K+ Kalshi contracts)
- 5¢ contracts lose 60%+ of capital (Favorite-Longshot Bias, Bürgi et al.)
- Kalshi exhibited 40.1% lower MAE than institutional consensus on inflation events
- Polymarket 2025 volume: $21.5B
- Kalshi taker fee formula: round_up(0.07 × contracts × price × (1 − price))

## Core Strategies
1. The Free Donut — exploiting Kalshi's collateral return on MECE bracket markets (5x capital efficiency)
2. The 88-Cent Rule — buying mispriced NO contracts at 88¢+ (true probability ~92%, +$0.04 EV)
3. Maker Edge — using post_only:true to avoid taker fees (32% vs 10% loss gap)
4. 72% NO Harvesting — systematic fading of overpriced YES on Polymarket
5. EMOS Anchoring — using ensemble weather models vs single forecasts (30% better accuracy)

## Platform Coverage
- Kalshi: CFTC-regulated DCM, all 50 US states legal, USD settlement, NWS/BLS/FOMC resolution data
- Polymarket: Offshore (Panama), USDC on Polygon, UMA oracle, $21.5B 2025 volume, US-restricted main platform

## Content Pages
- / — Main landing page
- /learn/ — 8-chapter curriculum hub
- /learn/what-is-a-prediction-market.html — Ch.1.1: What is a prediction market
- /learn/how-contracts-work.html — Ch.1.2: How contracts work
- /learn/prediction-markets-vs-sports-betting.html — Ch.1.3: vs sports betting
- /learn/why-traders-lose.html — Ch.1.4: Why 99% of traders lose
- /learn/kalshi-explained.html — Ch.2.1: Kalshi explained
- /learn/polymarket-explained.html — Ch.2.2: Polymarket explained
- /learn/kalshi-vs-polymarket.html — Ch.2.3: Kalshi vs Polymarket
- /learn/kalshi-strategies.html — Ch.5: Kalshi strategies (coming soon)
- /learn/polymarket-strategies.html — Ch.6: Polymarket strategies (coming soon)
- /learn/risk-management.html — Ch.7: Risk & bankroll management (coming soon)
- /learn/advanced-algorithmic.html — Ch.8: Advanced & algorithmic trading (coming soon)

## Key Academic Sources
- Bürgi, Deng & Whelan (2026) — 313,972 Kalshi contracts, Maker/Taker performance, FLB analysis
- Reichenbach & Walther (2025) — 124 million trades, profitability analysis
- Rasooly & Rozzi (2025) — real-money vs play-money market accuracy
- Snowberg & Wolfers (2010) — Favorite-Longshot Bias across 5 million horse races
- Sonnemann, Camerer, Fox & Langer (2013) — Partition Dependence Bias
- Ali (1977) — original FLB documentation in pari-mutuel horse racing

Polymarket affiliate link: https://polymarket.com/?r=beatpoly
